calendar.matters:
Motion To Vacate
Off Calendar Aug-30-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jane Feng, 415-876-6703 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Georgetta Beck, Esq. 415-485-2200 of Miller Hauser Law Group LLP for Defendant through Court Call.
Mandarin Court Interpreter Aidong Ni assists Plaintiff.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff presents documentation to the Court, argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF JANE FENG's Motion To Vacate.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff Jane Feng's motion to vacate the July 18, 2018 judgment in favor of defendant David Yang is denied. Ms. Feng fails to provide any legal basis for vacating the judgment.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Brenda Crow, CSR#10503, bcrow@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reasonable Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar May-04-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JANE FENG, TEL. NO. 1-415-876-6703, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. JERRY R. HAUSER OF THE MILLER HAUSER LAW GROUP, LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-485-2200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant David Yang's motion for attorney's fees and costs is granted in its entirety. Mr. Yang is awarded $7,184.36 in fees and costs against plaintiff Jane Feng. Mr. Yang is entitled to an award of fees and costs as the prevailing party on his anti-SLAPP motion. All fees and costs he seeks were reasonably incurred and the hourly rate and time spent was also reasonable. Ms. Feng's arguments focus on the merits of the case rather than Mr. Yang's entitlement to fees and costs and the reasonable amount thereof, which are the only issues on this motion. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-28-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jane Feng, in pro per, Tel. 415.876.6703, for plaintiffs;
Jerry R. Hauser, of Miller Hauser Law Group, LLP, Tel. 415.485.2200, for defendant Yang, appearing via CourtCall;
Interpreter: Daniel Hsueh, Tel. 408.707.7524, Santa Clara Court, Cert. 100732.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DAVID YANG's anti-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT as to the sole cause of action for fraud alleged in the complaint filed on January 18, 2017, by plaintiff Jane Feng is granted. Mr. Yang has shown that the fraud cause of action arises from protected activity: Mr. Yang's filing of documents in this court in a prior case. The commercial speech exception set forth in CCP 425.17(c) is inapplicable. Ms. Feng has failed to show a probability of prevailing on her fraud claim because that claim is barred by the litigation privilege codified at CCP 47(b). Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
